findings.title: Positive-balance token mechanics let anyone skim unaccounted surplus from the pair

findings.title: Balance-decreasing tokens can desynchronize reserves, DoS swaps, and force LP losses on sync

findings.title: Cached EIP-712 domain separator allows permit replay after a chain split or chain-id change

best_agent_match.title: Cached EIP-712 domain separator allows permit replay after a chain-id change or fork

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can steal already-transferred liquidity deposits by calling `mint` first
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended low-level pair pattern inherited from Uniswap V2: token transfer and `mint` must be finalized atomically, typically through a router. It only affects users or integrations that split the deposit flow across transactions, so it is integration misuse rather than a standalone protocol flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can steal pending withdrawals once LP tokens have been transferred into the pair
rejected_candidates.reason: This is likewise the expected low-level `burn` flow: LP tokens are transferred to the pair and burned in the same transaction. A front-run is only possible for naive multi-transaction integrations or direct misuse, not during the intended atomic interaction pattern.

rejected_candidates.title: Pair can be re-initialized by the factory because `initialize` lacks a one-time guard
rejected_candidates.reason: Only the `factory` address can call `initialize`. Without evidence that the factory itself exposes an attacker-reachable path, this depends on privileged misuse or compromise of a trusted component rather than an independent permissionless vulnerability in the pair.
